default_pipeline.name: q15_fixed_k_holdout_feedback_physical_validation
default_pipeline.description: Fixed K=32768 AGP support selected from a bounded nested-commutator Krylov pool; fifteen feedback iterations add hard residual equations from a larger Q=65536 residual holdout pool without growing AGP outputs.

baseline_neural.description: Train or reuse the retained SiLU baseline checkpoint, then warm-start the PAU feedback model from that checkpoint. This makes the PAU benchmark reproducible even from a cleaned q15 folder.

support_swap.description: Fixed-K support-swap curriculum: after each trained round, replace weak low-importance AGP strings with hard residual-derived candidates while keeping K unchanged.

agp_calibration.description: Joint curriculum training of a global AGP scale and soft Pauli gates against the projected Euler-Lagrange residual. It is active from the baseline run through every holdout-feedback round and does not use final ground-state energy, fidelity, or observable targets.

schedule_optimization.description: Trainable scheduling function lambda(t)=lambda_0(t)+tau^2(1-tau)^2*A_sched*tanh(u_NN(t)), with lambda_0 equal to the current sin^2 schedule and A_sched=2.4 chosen to keep lambda within [0,1] for this base schedule. Endpoint values and endpoint derivatives are enforced by construction.

physical_validation.description: Post-training q15 statevector diagnostic comparing final observables under no counterdiabatic drive, the Kipu/DQFM-style l=1 nested-commutator CD approximator, and the jointly calibrated learned sparse AGP.
